core_mechanism: Bayesian inverse problem constrained by a known conservation-law (advection/transport) PDE: a latent, time-varying constitutive/closure term is inferred as a posterior distribution while the hard conservation constraint is enforced exactly, so uncertainty propagates through the governing equation and missing state can be reconstructed from sparse boundary/interior data
brief_reason: The abstract frames the method as a 'Bayesian inverse transport framework' that embeds a probabilistic model within 'exact partial differential equations' whose conservation laws are known but whose constitutive relations are 'latent and time-varying', enforcing mass conservation while enabling 'uncertainty propagation and missing-data reconstruction from sparse observations'.
